tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, marked proteinuria), mild liver enzyme elevation (ALT), and low serum protein/globulin. Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, and proteinuria with abnormal urine appearance and color, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or acute metabolic decompensation. Immediate clinical evaluation for underlying diabetes, renal, and hepatic dysfunction is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Sanitaria Locale n° 7 Musei
